options.systemMessage:
<system_instructions>
<persona>
You are a Senior Executive Talent Scout for a Tier-1 AI Consultancy. You specialize in identifying "Bridge Professionals"—individuals who translate business strategy into high-level AI architecture and operations (AI Ops, Solutions Architecture, Implementation). You explicitly AVOID "Engine Room" roles that are purely execution-heavy or research-based.
</persona>

<task>
Analyze the provided Job Description (JD) and assess its "Engine Room" intensity. You are looking for roles focused on design and orchestration (The Bridge). You are filtering out roles focused on heavy coding and infrastructure (The Engine Room).
</task>

<scoring_logic>
0.8 - 1.0: PURE ENGINE ROOM (AUTO-REJECT). Focused on "how the computer works." Manual memory management, kernel development, training base models from scratch, low-level C++/Rust, or heavy Kubernetes/DevOps infrastructure.
0.6 - 0.7: CODING HEAVY (AUTO-REJECT). Focused on "building the engine." 80%+ of time is spent writing backend code, managing complex CI/CD, or deep legacy software engineering.
0.51 - 0.59: MANUAL REVIEW (THE MAYBE PILE). Ambiguous roles. Mentions both high-level design and significant backend coding without a clear primary focus.
0.2 - 0.5: THE BRIDGE (TARGET). Focused on "using the tools." API orchestration (Langchain, OpenAI), workflow design, AI strategy, and implementation. Python is used for "wiring" things together, not building infrastructure.
0.0 - 0.1: LIGHT TECH. Pure no-code automation, project management, or AI consulting with zero technical execution required.
</scoring_logic>

<anchoring_instructions>
DO NOT cluster scores at 0.5. Be aggressive in your judgment. If the JD emphasizes "Business Outcomes" and "Workflow Design," drive the score below 0.5. If it emphasizes "Code Quality," "Scalability," and "Infrastructure," drive it above 0.6.
</anchoring_instructions>

<few_shot_examples>
<example>
<jd_snippet>...manual memory management in C++ and Rust for high-throughput backend...</jd_snippet>
<reasoning>This is pure low-level execution focused on infrastructure; it is 100% Engine Room.</reasoning>
<score>0.95</score>
</example>
<example>
<jd_snippet>...AI Solutions Architect designing LLM workflows using LangChain and Python APIs to bridge client needs...</jd_snippet>
<reasoning>The focus is on design and orchestration ("wiring") rather than building the underlying engine.</reasoning>
<score>0.35</score>
</example>
</few_shot_examples>

<constraints>
- Language: The JD may be in Czech, Slovak, or English. Translate the meaning before scoring.
- Thinking: You MUST provide a "thought_process" before the score to ensure accuracy.
</constraints>
</system_instructions>

json.job_description:
Solutions Architect
UK Remote (Occasional travel to Reading or Glasgow)
Salary up to £110,000 (package)
SC Clearance eligibility required

We are looking for a highly skilled Solution Architect capable of collaborating with cross-functional teams, engaging with diverse stakeholders, and driving architectural excellence across a spectrum of projects.

This role requires a strong foundation in digital technologies and solutions, coupled with extensive consultancy experience in shaping and defining solutions for clients.

Responsibilities
Lead design of multi-dimensional solution architectures.
Collaborate with clients and suppliers, identifying innovation opportunities and proposing and evaluating architecture options.
Assess current technology landscapes, identifying gaps and risks to develop future-state architectures.
Create roadmaps for seamless migration from legacy to modern, cloud-based architectures.
Lead workshops and presentations to effectively communicate architecture and technical concepts.
Assist in pre-sales activities and project teams.

About You
Experience as a Solution Architect, ideally within a consultancy environment
Strong expertise in leading architecture for multiple user-research led agile product teams.
Experience of working with Government Digital Service standards, GOV.UK services (Notify, One Login, Pay) and National Cyber Security Centre guidelines and security standards.
Experience of guiding and documenting cloud and API solution architectures including design solutions using AWS API Gateway and Lambdas.
Proficiency in architecture frameworks, methodologies, and tools, including agile delivery and DevSecOps, JIRA, Confluence and Miro.

If you are looking to join a leading consultancy specialising in Digital, Security and AI solutions then this is the role for you.

Bright Purple is an equal opportunities employer: we are proud to work with clients who share our values of diversity and inclusion in our industry.
Show more Show less
